audit_method: source-level static analysis (live URL inaccessible from this environment)

chrome-devtools-mcp (canonical).title: Browser Automation Meets Production Standards — Chrome DevTools MCP and the Governance Layer
chrome-devtools-mcp (canonical).description: Chrome DevTools MCP gives any compatible AI agent full browser control through a standards-based interface. That's powerful for prototypes. For production, you need a governance layer. Here's where Molecule AI fits in.
h2s: The Problem With Raw CDP Access
Real-World Use Cases the Governance Layer Enables

chrome-devtools-mcp-seo (variant).title: Give Your AI Agent a Real Browser: MCP + Chrome DevTools
chrome-devtools-mcp-seo (variant).description: Learn how to add browser automation to your AI agents using Chrome DevTools and the Model Context Protocol. Full Python code examples — no Puppeteer wrappers, no SaaS dependencies.
h1s: Give Your AI Agent a Real Browser: MCP + Chrome DevTools
Start the CDP session — connects to Chrome's remote debugging port
Attach browser tools as MCP tools on the agent
Set your browser MCP server endpoint via the platform API
h2s: The Chrome DevTools Protocol + MCP Bridge
Full Code Example: AI Agent That Researches Competitors

critical_issues: Meta description too long on canonical variant (218 chars, target <160)

SEO variant has 8 H1 tags (WCAG: should be exactly 1)

passed: Proper frontmatter (slug, date, tags) on both variants

Readability: avg sentence ~15-17 words (good for technical audience)

recommendations: Trim canonical description to ≤160 chars — current 218 chars will truncate in SERPs
Convert 7 extra H1s in SEO variant to H2 tags (WCAG compliance)
Verify live site canonical tag matches expected URL once site is accessible
Confirm og:image dimensions (1200×630 required for most social cards)
